hero.titleBefore: Turn manga into a story you can
hero.lead: MangaShift reads every panel, follows the original flow, adds narration and motion, then renders a finished video. No timeline. No manual cut.
hero.heroImageAlt: Editorial collage representing MangaShift motion direction

about.lead: MangaShift is not an image generator. It is a production pipeline for artwork that already exists. The page remains the source; AI handles the repetitive work between scan and screen.

principles.title: Keep the composition
principles.text: The camera follows the artist’s panel order instead of inventing a new scene.

principles.title: Direct the voice
principles.text: Dialogue, narration and pauses are shaped around what happens on the page.

principles.title: Publish the result
principles.text: The output is a video made for watching, sharing and iterating.

about.imageAlt: Editorial collage representing the MangaShift visual direction
about.figcaption: The artwork stays recognizable. Motion and audio support the reading rhythm instead of covering it up.

how.title: From silent page to narrated
how.lead: Four stages replace the usual editing timeline. The illustration stays central; each step adds one clear layer to the final sequence.

steps.title: Upload
steps.text: Add a page, chapter or vertical webtoon. MangaShift accepts the artwork as it is.

steps.title: Understand
steps.text: The pipeline finds panels, reading direction, speech bubbles and scene changes.

steps.title: Direct
steps.text: Dialogue gets a cast, narration gets timing, and each panel receives a camera move.

steps.title: Render
steps.text: Video, audio and subtitles are composed into a finished file ready to publish.

features.title: The production work, without the production
features.lead: Each feature exists to shorten the path from finished artwork to a watchable, narrated format.

items.title: Narration that follows the scene
items.text: The script is built from panel order and dialogue context, so the voice follows the page instead of reading a text dump.

items.title: Panel-aware camera
items.text: Framing respects manga and manhwa layouts, including right-to-left reading.

items.title: Captions included
items.text: Speech, narration and subtitles stay synchronized with the final cut.

items.title: One finished export
items.text: Get a video file for horizontal or vertical publishing without rebuilding the edit.

usecases.headingBefore: Built around the way you want to
usecases.intro: Choose a point of view. The active panel slides into place and shows how the same pipeline changes for readers, creators and publishing teams.

modes.label: Readers
modes.title: A calmer way to follow the story.
modes.text: Narration, readable captions and panel-led motion turn a chapter into a format that works on a commute, on a TV or when reading feels tiring.

modes.label: Creators
modes.title: Move from finished pages to a finished cut.
modes.text: Use the artwork you already made. MangaShift prepares narration, camera motion and timing, then returns a video ready for review and publishing.

modes.label: Publishers
modes.title: Give existing stories a new screen.
modes.text: Adapt selected titles into watchable previews and narrated episodes without rebuilding the production workflow for every chapter.

gallery.headingBefore: A visual system with room to
gallery.paragraph: These are art-direction studies, not fake product results. They define the paper, ink, composition and pacing that the beta interface will use.

demo.title: A page that knows when to
demo.lead: This sample shows the direction: panel-led framing, narration timing and motion composed into one watchable sequence.

powered.leadAfter: — each pass handled by a model tuned for the job.
powered.body: We stay model-agnostic on purpose. As the field moves, so does the engine — you keep the same upload-in, reel-out workflow while the parts underneath only get sharper.
powered.outroAfter: The names under the hood will change — the result on screen only gets better.

stats.title: Built to
stats.lead: One pipeline carries a whole chapter from silent page to narrated reel — measured, repeatable, and ready for release.

metrics.label: Pages per run
metrics.note: A full chapter, read in order, panel by panel — no manual cuts.

metrics.label: Languages
metrics.note: Narration and subtitles generated across scripts, left- or right-to-left.

metrics.label: Export to video
metrics.note: Upload in, a narrated reel out — MP4 with synced audio and motion.

metrics.label: AI voices
metrics.note: A distinct voice per character, plus a narrator — cast automatically.

pricing.title: Free while it's
pricing.lead: One plan is open today — the whole pipeline, no card, no catch. Pro and Studio land at public launch.
pricing.noteBefore: Figures shown for paid tiers are indicative only and
pricing.noteAfter: . Final pricing is set before public launch — beta members keep their founding terms.

plans.name: Free
plans.tagline: The whole pipeline, while we build it together.
features: AI narration with a single voice

plans.name: Pro
plans.tagline: For creators shipping reels on a schedule.

plans.name: Studio
plans.tagline: For teams and publishers at volume.

faq.title: A few clear
faq.lead: MangaShift is still in active development. These answers describe the beta direction without pretending unfinished parts are already final.

items.question: What does MangaShift create?
items.answer: A narrated video built from your manga or manhwa pages. The pipeline detects panel order, prepares the script, adds voices and motion, then renders the result.

items.question: Does it redraw or replace the artwork?
items.answer: No. The current direction keeps the supplied page as the visual source. MangaShift adds framing, timing, narration, audio and subtitles around it.

items.question: Is the beta free?
items.answer: Yes. The private beta is free while the workflow and output quality are being tested with early users.

items.question: Which languages will be available?
items.answer: English is the first interface and narration target. Polish is prepared as the next site language, with additional narration languages planned after the core pipeline is stable.

items.question: When will I get access?
items.answer: Invites will be sent in small waves. Joining the list is the best way to receive an access email when a new beta cohort opens.

items.question: What kind of pages work best?
items.answer: Clear, legible scans of manga or vertical manhwa. The pipeline reads panel layout and speech bubbles, so high-resolution pages with readable lettering give the most reliable framing and narration.

items.question: How does it handle right-to-left reading?
items.answer: Reading direction is detected per title. Traditional manga is followed right-to-left and top-to-bottom, while vertical manhwa is read as a continuous scroll, so the camera moves in the order a human reader would.

items.question: Where do the voices come from?
items.answer: Narration and dialogue use text-to-speech tuned to scene context. The goal is a consistent cast and pacing that supports the page, not a literal read-out of every line.

items.question: Can I edit the result?
items.answer: The beta returns a finished video rather than an editing timeline. Control over framing, voices and timing is expanding as the pipeline matures; early users help decide which controls land first.

items.question: What formats does it export?
items.answer: A standard video file in horizontal or vertical aspect, with synchronized subtitles. The same chapter can be rendered for a widescreen player or a vertical short.

items.question: Do I keep the rights to my work?
items.answer: Yes. You only grant what is needed to process the pages you upload and generate your video. MangaShift does not claim ownership of your artwork or the rendered output.

beta.lead: Leave your email to receive a beta invitation. You will hear from us when there is a real build to test, not another generic newsletter.
beta.consent: I agree to receive email about MangaShift beta access and product updates.
beta.successMessage: Thanks. Your beta request has been recorded.
beta.errorMessage: The request could not be sent. Please try again.
beta.disclaimer: Only useful product emails. Unsubscribe whenever you want.

footer.intro: MangaShift is building a direct path from static manga and manhwa to narrated video, while keeping the original composition at the center.
footer.brandTagline: Static manga and manhwa, directed into voice, pacing and motion.

features.titleBefore: A local studio for comics you
features.lead: MangaShift is a desktop comic reader and a toolkit of AI features for translating, narrating and adapting material you hold the rights to. Your projects stay on your machine.

items.title: Local comic reader
items.text: Read your library on Windows with a focused, panel-aware reader. MangaShift is not a content store and ships no comics of its own.

items.title: OCR & translation
items.text: Extract text and translate it with local models or your own API keys (BYOK). You stay in control of every provider you connect.

items.title: Text-to-speech
items.text: Turn dialogue and narration into speech for material you are allowed to process, with voices from your configured providers.

items.title: Frame animation
items.text: Add motion to panels and assemble scenes for legally held material — a path from static page toward a moving adaptation.

items.title: Audio & video export
items.text: Export audio, video or motion comic when you hold the rights for that use. Results render locally and stay yours.

items.title: BYOK & local projects
items.text: Bring your own keys for Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, ElevenLabs, DeepL and more. Keys live in your system credential store; projects stay on disk.

features.note: MangaShift does not host your files, does not provide comics, and is not a way around copyright. Importing a comic does not by itself grant the right to adapt, narrate or publish it.

pricing.lead: MangaShift will offer a Free plan plus paid Monthly and Annual plans with a 7-day card trial. Final prices, currency and billing dates are confirmed at checkout before you pay.
pricing.trialNote: 7 days free, then [[CENA]] per month. Card required. Cancel before the trial ends to avoid the charge.
pricing.draftNote: Prices below are placeholders. Checkout is not enabled until pricing, operator details and consent checkboxes are final.

plans.name: Free
plans.desc: Basic access to MangaShift with feature or usage limits.

plans.name: Monthly
plans.desc: Fuller access to MangaShift features. Renews automatically. Cancel anytime — access lasts until the end of the paid period.

plans.name: Annual
plans.desc: Fuller access to MangaShift features, billed yearly. Renews automatically unless checkout states otherwise.

pricing.byok: MangaShift's price does not cover the cost of third-party API accounts (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, ElevenLabs, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, DeepL). You pay those providers directly under their own terms.

download.lead: MangaShift ships first as a Windows desktop app via GitHub Releases. A Google account and an internet connection are required at least for sign-in, licensing and updates.
download.availability: The public installer arrives with the beta. Until then, request access and you'll get the link first.
download.note: Support for macOS or Linux may be added later. For now, MangaShift targets Windows.

contact.lead: Questions about MangaShift, your account or a legal matter — reach the right inbox below, or use the form. Formal complaints and refunds go by email or the form, not Discord.
contact.formNotice: The contact backend opens with the beta. For now, email us directly at the address above.

legalHub.lead: Drafts of the MangaShift legal documents. They are being finalised with a lawyer and will be published before paid plans launch. Documents are kept in Polish for now and will be translated later.
legalHub.draftNote: These documents are working drafts (V3) and are not yet legally binding.
